description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:19.03.2 image with a docker:19.03.2-dind service and a global before_script that logs in to $CI_REGISTRY with $CI_REGISTRY_USER and $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD, and define three stages: test, prepare, and build. In the test stage add a test-ci-generator job that runs on python:3.7-alpine, executes a before_script to cd ci-generator, check python version, install virtualenv, create and activate a venv, install requirements.txt, caches .cache/pip and ci-generator/venv/, then runs coverage run -m unittest tests/test*.py, coverage report, and coverage html, and stores the generated htmlcov directory as an artifact. In the prepare stage add a prepare_2019_1_3f1 job that runs ci/prepare.sh, retries twice, sets Unity‑specific variables (BUILD=f1, DOWNLOAD_URL, SHA1, TAG=2019.1.3f1, VERSION=2019.1.3, etc.), and uploads licence_request.alf as an artifact. In the build stage define a hidden .build template that retries twice and runs ci/build.sh, and create six platform jobs (unity_2019_1_3f1-android, unity_2019_1_3f1-ios, unity_2019_1_3f1-linux-il2cpp, unity_2019_1_3f1-mac, unity_2019_1_3f1-webgl, unity_2019_1_3f1-windows) that inherit the .build script, each with retry 2 and their own variables for COMPONENTS, DOCKERFILE_NAME, TAG, RELEASE_NOTES, RELEASE_URL, etc., all using the global docker image. No special rules are defined, so all jobs run on any branch or tag.
